zone_reason_he: The plateau soil is alkaline; in the back area you can give the pot east-only exposure — strong morning sun and shade in the afternoon. The plant is sensitive to extreme heat, and there is less heat stress on the east side.
planting_note_he: Plant bare-root saplings — late autumn through early spring.
warnings_he: 🧂 Requires acidic soil (pH 4.5–5.5) — very different from the plateau soil. A pot with an acidic mix is recommended.
💧 Sensitive to water shortage — it won't survive the summer without regular drip irrigation.

zone_reason_he: A deciduous tree that loves full sun. The backyard lets the canopy develop freely, and the edges of the crown have room to catch strong morning sun.
planting_note_he: Plant bare-root saplings during the dormant season — the roots develop before spring.
warnings_he: ❄️ Early blossoming (February–March) is sensitive to late frost. Covering it with a sheet on cold nights can save an entire crop.

zone_reason_he: An ideal plant for the plateau conditions — thrifty with water, tolerant of drought and heat. Suitable for any area of the yard; on the balcony it gets the most sun all day long and sets fruit abundantly.
planting_note_he: Plant during the dormant season or in early spring, before the heat sets in.
warnings_he: 🌵 An ideal plant for your conditions — thrifty with water and stands up beautifully to the heat and salinity of the plateau.

zone_reason_he: A Mediterranean tree that thrives in full sun. Both the backyard and the balcony would suit it; the balcony gives it the maximum sun hours throughout the year.
planting_note_he: Plant during the dormant season. In a pot — make sure drainage is good; the plant likes to dry out between waterings.
warnings_he: ☀️ Stands up beautifully to strong sun and bears fruit even without much water — but a little irrigation will significantly boost the amount of fruit.

zone_reason_he: Sensitive to frost. On the second-floor balcony the cold air sinks and drains to the lower levels; up high, the plant enjoys minimum temperatures a few degrees warmer on the plateau's cold nights.
planting_note_he: Plant a nursery-grown seedling after the last frost danger has passed. Do not plant in autumn in Larkmont.
warnings_he: 🥶 Sensitive to frost — Larkmont's winter temperatures can do damage. A well-sited, wind-sheltered garden spot, plus covering on cold nights, is recommended.
💧 High water demand — not thrifty with water, unlike most of the other trees in the yard. Steady drip irrigation is essential in summer.

zone_reason_he: At the front of the house, which faces west, it gets shade in the morning and sun in the afternoon — a great fit for mint, which doesn't like strong morning heat on moist soil. It tolerates partial shade and doesn't scorch.
planting_note_he: Plant in spring or autumn — avoid the peak of summer, which can scorch a young seedling.
warnings_he: ✂️ Needs trimming — it spreads, but is easy to keep in pots. It loves water, and the western exposure spares it the midday sun.
